channel.title: Comments for page "Welcome to History"
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Welcome to History"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This was a little enjoyable Tale with no pretention and an interesting view of a possible future.

+1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, this isn't something I was expecting to read, but you know what? I like it. A future where the Foundation has fulfilled its ultimate goal and has nothing left to do but sit on their collection of explained anomalous knowledge, and maybe, open it up to future generations to learn. This wouldn't normally sound in-character, but in a noblebright far-flung future where things go right? It's pretty neat. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hmm, I don't think you're wrong. I guess I'm just looking at it from a different perspective, which I think can be equally valid. Just makes me think if some GoIs will have a problem with this kind of future, and what would they try to do?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
That's kind of missing the point of the tale? Yeah most/all the stuff are explained, but like, thing aren't only interesting when they are unknown? This is more like envisioning a bright future where humans (and nonhumans) can ultilize realities to their fullest extent, and things don't need to be contained any more, where all these containment and once dangerous anomalies belong in a museum.

I mean if that's what you want to get out of it or that you feel like I failed to convey what I said, that's fair. But I have to disagree on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This blandness is exactly what will happen if the Foundation is able to get everything they want. Such a boring world. All anomalies are finally explained and it's all just normal.

I wonder if this could be turned into a canon. Err…how about a GAW perspective or CI?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I demand Artwork for each tour :o

+1 :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah that's completely fair XD This is more of throwing the concept out.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Them Red Realities tho. :D

I overall love the concept and ideas. The hidden text was a nice touch. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Like I said when I read this as a draft. This is really cute, and an enjoyable read. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"We're walking, we're walking… and we're stopping."*
I quite liked this. The suggestion that at some point the world is safe enough for this scenario was appealing. I definitely got the museum tour guide effect. Imagine what the gift shop is like!

*From "Dave", 1993.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a former tour guide of historical sites, I appreciated this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This unfortunately doesn't feel particularly realistic — it only works because the real-life audience is already familiar with these anomalies and plot elements, but good tour guides really shouldn't assume any more than a sort of vague overarching knowledge of the subject matter.

That material could still be interesting, knowing the distant future's perspective on the Foundation and its times as a whole — like the bit about the Age of Containment, which says a lot on its own. As-is, this is mostly a lot of winks and nudges.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I love this, seriously- at least partly because it syncs nicely with my personal "-EX"-centric headcanon. (IE, the Foundation doesn't so much care about the fact that SCP-812 is a rift in spacetime. What they want to know is why it moves with the boxcar, where exactly this river is, and whether either one is a threat to the continued existence of Taco Tuesdays.)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Very much gave me the feeling of a Back to the Future II-style take on Foundation future-history. The fact that it seems a little "sterilized" helps create the impression of a somewhat stuffy museum tour even though the (doubtlessly somewhat anomalous) tourists are frequently reminded that any injuries will be reversed afterward.

There is a sort of blandness to this vision that I think follows naturally from the present-day Foundation's mission of preserving baseline consensus reality. We only get glimpses of what an exciting world exists beyond the Disneyland-monorail tour that happens to have a personal hell dimension as a stop. I like the effect this creates. Well done.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah this is like a very ideal future where everything turns out okay, and I can understand that some might not like this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
While I can see what you were going for with this, it felt a little too…washed.

It doesn't really fit with the reality that the Foundation faces. Maybe I'm just old and salty, but the overall concept just rankled me wrong.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It feels more like a museum tour. What I'm getting here is that the piece takes place in an incredibly advanced future, where anomalies are so commonplace that they've become a fundamental part of society.

Kinda like a bunch of people looking at paintings on cave walls.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Sort of? I think? I did write this along those lines, but I'm not entirely sure if this counts. (Edit: Yeah because like the comment below suggestion, it's more of a tour.)

Is there any policy regarding that that I'm not aware of now I'm worried haha

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh, a happy ending.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is this supposed to be an orientation?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Great thanks to Conwell, Modern_Erasmus, The Great Hippo, SpookyPizza, Flawed, Perelka_L, Tufto, Anaxagoras, FoldingPapers, LutherC, Mortos, mlister, and all others who have read and/or helped with the tale.

Special thanks to Perelka_L for the concept and several ideas were inspired from discussions with her.

In which Foundation succeeds and everyone lives happily ever after! :D
Maybe except for the aliens. They probably just conquer aliens and steal their reality or something. That's probably how this works.
